title: Activision announces open beta dates for Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 in late August
summary: The multiplayer beta will take place from August 28 to September 1, with early access for those who pre-order the game.

title: Physical video game sales on PlayStation hit historic lows in the United States
summary: Only seven titles have sold over 100,000 physical units so far this year, highlighting the dominance of digital format.

title: DC announces DCKO, a free-to-play fighting game for mobile devices
summary: The title, developed by an external studio, will come to iOS and Android in 2027 and deviates from expectations of Injustice 3.

title: Splatoon Raiders now available for Nintendo Switch 2 with mixed reviews
summary: The game combines Splatoon mechanics with Borderlands elements, generating mixed opinions on its single-player campaign and multiplayer.

title: Forza Horizon 5 creative director criticizes Xbox Game Pass's impact on the industry
summary: In several interviews, Mike Brown asserts that the subscription service cannibalized sales and contributed to mass layoffs in the sector.

title: British retail chain GAME enters administration with £16 million debt
summary: The company could not cope with digital competition, changes in consumer habits, and uncertainty from Brexit.

title: Nintendo requests dismissal of class action lawsuit over Switch price increase due to tariffs
summary: The company argues that tariffs are assumed by Nintendo and that consumers are not entitled to a refund.

title: Jennifer English leaves lead role in Tides of Annihilation
summary: The actress, known for her work in Baldur's Gate 3 and Expedition 33, withdraws from the project for personal reasons not yet disclosed.

title: Palworld reaches nearly one million concurrent players on Steam after 1.0 launch
summary: The indie game continues its impressive trajectory, approaching the mark of one million concurrent users.

title: Activision reveals setting for Call of Duty movie, which will not be World War II
summary: The film adaptation will focus on a modern conflict, according to internal sources.

title: Unity announces its new Unity 7 engine with release planned for first quarter of 2027
summary: The company highlights that Unity 7 will be a direct evolution of Unity 6 and will address generative AI ethically.

title: Judge rules a temporary 14-day pause in Paramount and Warner Bros. merger
summary: The court order seeks to investigate potential antitrust law violations in the operation.

title: Details leaked of canceled Kingdom Hearts mobile game that could be linked to Kingdom Hearts 4
summary: Leaked material reveals concepts and scenes that could have been part of the fourth main title.

title: Wreckreation 2 moves forward developed by Burnout veterans after closure of their original studio
summary: Those responsible for the game promise new mechanics and a renewed focus on vehicular destruction.

title: Neill Blomkamp releases an AI-generated film and receives negative reviews
summary: The director of District 9 experimented with AI to create a feature film, but the work did not convince critics.

title: Cliff Bleszinski talks about his career, alcoholism, and a possible return to video game development
summary: The creator of Gears of War reviews his successes and failures, and leaves the door open to returning to the industry.

title: Avengers: Doomsday trailer divides fans and generates theories about Robert Downey Jr.'s return
summary: The first images show a dark and apocalyptic tone, and suggest that Downey could play Doctor Doom.

title: FBI arrests 21-year-old for stealing over $200,000 with infected games on Steam
summary: The accused sold malicious titles that stole bank data and spent the money on UberEats gift cards.

title: NVIDIA reveals the controls developers will have for DLSS 5
summary: The new version of DLSS will include two main sliders to adjust the artificial intelligence filter.

title: Midgar Studio, the studio behind Edge of Eternity, is liquidated after failing to find a buyer
summary: The bankruptcy of publisher Nacon and lack of investor lead to the definitive closure of the studio.

title: Gamescom 2026 sells out exhibitor space and adds Konami and Tencent Games
summary: The German trade show will have a record number of exhibitors in 2026, including new major participants.
